channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2025 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Anyonization of Bosons in One Dimension: An Effective Swap Model
item.description:
Author(s): Botao Wang, Amit Vashisht, Yanliang Guo, Sudipta Dhar, Manuele Landini, Hanns-Christoph Nägerl, and Nathan Goldman

Anyons emerge as elementary excitations in low-dimensional quantum systems and exhibit behavior distinct from bosons or fermions. In one dimension, anyons can arise from unconventional scattering processes or density-dependent hopping on a lattice. Here, we introduce a novel framework for realizing …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 253403] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Botao Wang, Amit Vashisht, Yanliang Guo, Sudipta Dhar, Manuele Landini, Hanns-Christoph Nägerl, and Nathan Goldman

Anyons emerge as elementary excitations in low-dimensional quantum systems and exhibit behavior distinct from bosons or fermions. In one dimension, anyons can arise from unconventional scattering processes or density-dependent hopping on a lattice. Here, we introduce a novel framework for realizing …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 253403] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Anyonization of Bosons in One Dimension: An Effective Swap Model
item.dc:creator: Botao Wang, Amit Vashisht, Yanliang Guo, Sudipta Dhar, Manuele Landini, Hanns-Christoph Nägerl, and Nathan Goldman
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Superfluid Dome in the Spatially Modulated Two-Dimensional XY Model
item.description:
Author(s): Feng-Feng Song, Aditya Chugh, Hanggai Nuomin, Naoki Kawashima, and Alexander Wietek

In strongly correlated electron systems, superconductivity and charge density waves often coexist in close proximity, suggesting a deeper relationship between these competing phases. Recent research indicates that these orders can intertwine, with the superconducting order parameter coupling to modu…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256001] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Feng-Feng Song, Aditya Chugh, Hanggai Nuomin, Naoki Kawashima, and Alexander Wietek

In strongly correlated electron systems, superconductivity and charge density waves often coexist in close proximity, suggesting a deeper relationship between these competing phases. Recent research indicates that these orders can intertwine, with the superconducting order parameter coupling to modu…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256001] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Superfluid Dome in the Spatially Modulated Two-Dimensional XY Model
item.dc:creator: Feng-Feng Song, Aditya Chugh, Hanggai Nuomin, Naoki Kawashima, and Alexander Wietek
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Phonons in Epitaxial ${\mathrm{DySi}}_{2}$: From the Bulk to Self-Organized Nanoislands and Nanowires
item.description:
Author(s): Svetoslav Stankov, Przemysław Piekarz, Anja Seiler, Dániel G. Merkel, Olga Bauder, Ramu Pradip, Tilo G. Baumbach, Aleksandr I. Chumakov, and Rudolf Rüffer

Using nuclear inelastic scattering on $^{161}\mathrm{Dy}$, we determined the Dy-partial phonon density of states of epitaxial ${\mathrm{DySi}}_{2}$ in bulk and self-organized nanoislands and nanowires (NWs) on vicinal Si(001) surface. Compared to the bulk, the nanoislands exhibit softening of the cr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256202] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Svetoslav Stankov, Przemysław Piekarz, Anja Seiler, Dániel G. Merkel, Olga Bauder, Ramu Pradip, Tilo G. Baumbach, Aleksandr I. Chumakov, and Rudolf Rüffer

Using nuclear inelastic scattering on Dy161, we determined the Dy-partial phonon density of states of epitaxial DySi2 in bulk and self-organized nanoislands and nanowires (NWs) on vicinal Si(001) surface. Compared to the bulk, the nanoislands exhibit softening of the crystal lattice, which is furthe…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256202] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Phonons in Epitaxial ${\mathrm{DySi}}_{2}$: From the Bulk to Self-Organized Nanoislands and Nanowires
item.dc:creator: Svetoslav Stankov, Przemysław Piekarz, Anja Seiler, Dániel G. Merkel, Olga Bauder, Ramu Pradip, Tilo G. Baumbach, Aleksandr I. Chumakov, and Rudolf Rüffer
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Intrinsic High Chern Numbers in Two-Dimensional ${\mathrm{M}}_{2}{\mathrm{X}}_{2}$ Materials
item.description:
Author(s): Zujian Dai, Xudong Zhu, and Lixin He

Despite sharing a common lattice structure, monolayer ${\mathrm{M}}_{2}{\mathrm{X}}_{2}$ compounds realize quantum anomalous Hall phases with distinct Chern numbers, a striking phenomenon that has not been fully explored. Combining first-principles calculations with symmetry analysis and tight-bindi…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256401] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zujian Dai, Xudong Zhu, and Lixin He

Despite sharing a common lattice structure, monolayer M2X2 compounds realize quantum anomalous Hall phases with distinct Chern numbers, a striking phenomenon that has not been fully explored. Combining first-principles calculations with symmetry analysis and tight-binding models, we identify two gen…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256401] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Intrinsic High Chern Numbers in Two-Dimensional ${\mathrm{M}}_{2}{\mathrm{X}}_{2}$ Materials
item.dc:creator: Zujian Dai, Xudong Zhu, and Lixin He
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Importance of Nonadiabatic Effects in Kohn Anomalies in 1D Metals
item.description:
Author(s): Enrico Marazzi, Samuel Poncé, Jean-Christophe Charlier, and Gian-Marco Rignanese

Kohn anomalies are kinks or dips in phonon dispersions which are pronounced in low-dimensional materials. We investigate the effects of nonadiabatic phonon self-energy on Kohn anomalies in one-dimensional metals by developing a model that analyzes how the adiabatic phonon frequency, electron effecti…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256402] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Enrico Marazzi, Samuel Poncé, Jean-Christophe Charlier, and Gian-Marco Rignanese

Kohn anomalies are kinks or dips in phonon dispersions which are pronounced in low-dimensional materials. We investigate the effects of nonadiabatic phonon self-energy on Kohn anomalies in one-dimensional metals by developing a model that analyzes how the adiabatic phonon frequency, electron effecti…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256402] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Importance of Nonadiabatic Effects in Kohn Anomalies in 1D Metals
item.dc:creator: Enrico Marazzi, Samuel Poncé, Jean-Christophe Charlier, and Gian-Marco Rignanese
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Chiral Wigner Crystal Phases Induced by Berry Curvature
item.description:
Author(s): Sandeep Joy, Leonid Levitov, and Brian Skinner

We study how Berry phase affects the Wigner crystal (WC) state of a two-dimensional electron system. We consider first a model of Bernal bilayer graphene with a perpendicular displacement field, and we show that Berry curvature leads to a new kind of WC state in which the electrons acquire a spontan…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256502] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sandeep Joy, Leonid Levitov, and Brian Skinner

We study how Berry phase affects the Wigner crystal (WC) state of a two-dimensional electron system. We consider first a model of Bernal bilayer graphene with a perpendicular displacement field, and we show that Berry curvature leads to a new kind of WC state in which the electrons acquire a spontan…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256502] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Chiral Wigner Crystal Phases Induced by Berry Curvature
item.dc:creator: Sandeep Joy, Leonid Levitov, and Brian Skinner
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantized Crystalline-Electromagnetic Responses in Insulators
item.description:
Author(s): Sachin Vaidya, André Grossi Fonseca, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Taylor L. Hughes, and Marin Soljačić

Nonsymmorphic momentum-space symmetries quantize electromagnetic-crystalline responses in insulators.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256602] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sachin Vaidya, André Grossi Fonseca, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Taylor L. Hughes, and Marin Soljačić

Nonsymmorphic momentum-space symmetries quantize electromagnetic-crystalline responses in insulators.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256602] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Quantized Crystalline-Electromagnetic Responses in Insulators
item.dc:creator: Sachin Vaidya, André Grossi Fonseca, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Taylor L. Hughes, and Marin Soljačić
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Fractional Topological States in Rhombohedral Multilayer Graphene Modulated by Kagome Superlattice
item.description:
Author(s): Yanran Shi, Bo Xie, Fengfan Ren, Xinyu Cai, Zhongqing Guo, Qiao Li, Xin Lu, Nicolas Regnault, Zhongkai Liu, and Jianpeng Liu

Rhombohedral multilayer graphene coupled with a patterned kagome superlattice produces various zero-magnetic-field fractional topological states.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256603] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yanran Shi, Bo Xie, Fengfan Ren, Xinyu Cai, Zhongqing Guo, Qiao Li, Xin Lu, Nicolas Regnault, Zhongkai Liu, and Jianpeng Liu

Rhombohedral multilayer graphene coupled with a patterned kagome superlattice produces various zero-magnetic-field fractional topological states.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256603] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Fractional Topological States in Rhombohedral Multilayer Graphene Modulated by Kagome Superlattice
item.dc:creator: Yanran Shi, Bo Xie, Fengfan Ren, Xinyu Cai, Zhongqing Guo, Qiao Li, Xin Lu, Nicolas Regnault, Zhongkai Liu, and Jianpeng Liu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Two-Dimensional ${J}_{1}\text{−}{J}_{2}$ Clock Model: Enhanced Symmetries, Emergent Orders, and Landau-Incompatible Transitions
item.description:
Author(s): Pulloor Kuttanikkad Vishnu, Abhishodh Prakash, Rajesh Narayanan, and Titas Chanda

We present a comprehensive study on the frustrated ${J}_{1}\text{−}{J}_{2}$ classical $q$-state clock model with even $q>4$ on a two-dimensional square lattice, revealing a rich ensemble of phases driven by competing interactions. In the unfrustrated regime (${J}_{1}>2{J}_{2}$), the model repr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256703] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pulloor Kuttanikkad Vishnu, Abhishodh Prakash, Rajesh Narayanan, and Titas Chanda

We present a comprehensive study on the frustrated J1−J2 classical q-state clock model with even q>4 on a two-dimensional square lattice, revealing a rich ensemble of phases driven by competing interactions. In the unfrustrated regime (J1>2J2), the model reproduces the standard clock model phe…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256703] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Two-Dimensional ${J}_{1}\text{−}{J}_{2}$ Clock Model: Enhanced Symmetries, Emergent Orders, and Landau-Incompatible Transitions
item.dc:creator: Pulloor Kuttanikkad Vishnu, Abhishodh Prakash, Rajesh Narayanan, and Titas Chanda
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Electrically Switchable Nonrelativistic Zeeman Spin Splittings in Collinear Antiferromagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Longju Yu, Hong Jian Zhao, Laurent Bellaiche, and Yanming Ma

Magnetic or electrical manipulation of electronic spin is elementary for spin-based logic, computing, and memory, where the latter is a low-power manipulation scheme. Rashba-like spin splittings stemming from spin-orbit interaction (SOI) enable electric-field manipulation of spin, but the relativist…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256704] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Longju Yu, Hong Jian Zhao, Laurent Bellaiche, and Yanming Ma

Magnetic or electrical manipulation of electronic spin is elementary for spin-based logic, computing, and memory, where the latter is a low-power manipulation scheme. Rashba-like spin splittings stemming from spin-orbit interaction (SOI) enable electric-field manipulation of spin, but the relativist…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256704] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Electrically Switchable Nonrelativistic Zeeman Spin Splittings in Collinear Antiferromagnets
item.dc:creator: Longju Yu, Hong Jian Zhao, Laurent Bellaiche, and Yanming Ma
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Sign Reversal and Nonmonotonicity of Chirality-Related Anomalous Hall Effect in Highly Conductive Metals
item.description:
Author(s): Ryunosuke Terasawa, Masafumi Udagawa, and Hiroaki Ishizuka

The nonmonotonic temperature dependence and sign reversal of chirality-related anomalous Hall effect in highly conductive metals are studied. Through the analysis of scattering rate, we find that the nonmonotonicity and sign reversal have two major origins: (1) competition between the contribution f…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256303] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ryunosuke Terasawa, Masafumi Udagawa, and Hiroaki Ishizuka

The nonmonotonic temperature dependence and sign reversal of chirality-related anomalous Hall effect in highly conductive metals are studied. Through the analysis of scattering rate, we find that the nonmonotonicity and sign reversal have two major origins: (1) competition between the contribution f…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256303] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.dc:title: Sign Reversal and Nonmonotonicity of Chirality-Related Anomalous Hall Effect in Highly Conductive Metals
item.dc:creator: Ryunosuke Terasawa, Masafumi Udagawa, and Hiroaki Ishizuka
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Observation of Shapiro Steps in the Charge Density Wave State Induced by Strain on a Piezoelectric Substrate
item.description:
Author(s): Koji Fujiwara, Takuya Kawada, Natsumi Nikaido, Jihoon Park, Nan Jiang, Shintaro Takada, and Yasuhiro Niimi

The current response of the charge density wave state in niobium triselenide shows Shapiro steps induced by strain from surface acoustic waves in the substrate.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256304] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Koji Fujiwara, Takuya Kawada, Natsumi Nikaido, Jihoon Park, Nan Jiang, Shintaro Takada, and Yasuhiro Niimi

The current response of the charge density wave state in niobium triselenide shows Shapiro steps induced by strain from surface acoustic waves in the substrate.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256304] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.dc:title: Observation of Shapiro Steps in the Charge Density Wave State Induced by Strain on a Piezoelectric Substrate
item.dc:creator: Koji Fujiwara, Takuya Kawada, Natsumi Nikaido, Jihoon Park, Nan Jiang, Shintaro Takada, and Yasuhiro Niimi
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Dynamically Tunable Hydrodynamic Transport in Boron-Nitride-Encapsulated Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Akash Gugnani, Aniket Majumdar, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, and Arindam Ghosh

Over the past decade, graphene has emerged as a promising candidate for exploring the viscous nature of electronic flow facilitated by the availability of extremely high-quality devices employing a graphene channel encapsulated within dielectric layers of hexagonal boron nitride (hBN). However, the …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256501] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Akash Gugnani, Aniket Majumdar, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, and Arindam Ghosh

Over the past decade, graphene has emerged as a promising candidate for exploring the viscous nature of electronic flow facilitated by the availability of extremely high-quality devices employing a graphene channel encapsulated within dielectric layers of hexagonal boron nitride (hBN). However, the …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256501] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.dc:title: Dynamically Tunable Hydrodynamic Transport in Boron-Nitride-Encapsulated Graphene
item.dc:creator: Akash Gugnani, Aniket Majumdar, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, and Arindam Ghosh

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Ab Initio Theory of Phonon Magnetic Moment Induced by Electron-Phonon Coupling in Magnetic Materials
item.description:
Author(s): Fuyi Wang, Xinqi Liu, Hong Sun, Huaiqiang Wang, Shuichi Murakami, Lifa Zhang, Haijun Zhang, and Dingyu Xing

Circularly polarized phonons, characterized by nonzero angular momenta and magnetic moments, have attracted extensive attention. However, a long-standing critical issue in this field is the lack of an approach to accurately calculate phonon magnetic moments resulting from electron-phonon coupling (E…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256701] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Fuyi Wang, Xinqi Liu, Hong Sun, Huaiqiang Wang, Shuichi Murakami, Lifa Zhang, Haijun Zhang, and Dingyu Xing

Circularly polarized phonons, characterized by nonzero angular momenta and magnetic moments, have attracted extensive attention. However, a long-standing critical issue in this field is the lack of an approach to accurately calculate phonon magnetic moments resulting from electron-phonon coupling (E…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256701] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.dc:title: Ab Initio Theory of Phonon Magnetic Moment Induced by Electron-Phonon Coupling in Magnetic Materials
item.dc:creator: Fuyi Wang, Xinqi Liu, Hong Sun, Huaiqiang Wang, Shuichi Murakami, Lifa Zhang, Haijun Zhang, and Dingyu Xing

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: $d$-Wave Flat Fermi Surface in Altermagnets Enables Maximum Charge-to-Spin Conversion
item.description:
Author(s): Junwen Lai, Tianye Yu, Peitao Liu, Long Liu, Guozhong Xing, Xing-Qiu Chen, and Yan Sun

Altermagnets combine antiferromagnetic order with ferromagnetlike spin splitting, a duality that unlocks ultrafast spin-dependent responses. This unique property creates unprecedented opportunities for spin-current generation, overcoming the intrinsic limitations of conventional spin-transfer and sp…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256702] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Junwen Lai, Tianye Yu, Peitao Liu, Long Liu, Guozhong Xing, Xing-Qiu Chen, and Yan Sun

Altermagnets combine antiferromagnetic order with ferromagnetlike spin splitting, a duality that unlocks ultrafast spin-dependent responses. This unique property creates unprecedented opportunities for spin-current generation, overcoming the intrinsic limitations of conventional spin-transfer and sp…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256702] Published Tue Dec 16, 2025
item.dc:title: $d$-Wave Flat Fermi Surface in Altermagnets Enables Maximum Charge-to-Spin Conversion
item.dc:creator: Junwen Lai, Tianye Yu, Peitao Liu, Long Liu, Guozhong Xing, Xing-Qiu Chen, and Yan Sun
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantum Kinetic Anatomy of Electron Angular Momenta Edge Accumulation
item.description:
Author(s): Thierry Valet, Henri Jaffrès, Vincent Cros, and Roberto Raimondi

Controlling electron spin and orbital degrees of freedom has been a major research focus over the past two decades, as it underpins the electrical manipulation of magnetization. Leveraging a recently introduced quantum kinetic theory of multiband systems [T. Valet and R. Raimondi, Phys. Rev. B 111, …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256301] Published Mon Dec 15, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Thierry Valet, Henri Jaffrès, Vincent Cros, and Roberto Raimondi

Controlling electron spin and orbital degrees of freedom has been a major research focus over the past two decades, as it underpins the electrical manipulation of magnetization. Leveraging a recently introduced quantum kinetic theory of multiband systems [T. Valet and R. Raimondi, Phys. Rev. B 111, …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256301] Published Mon Dec 15, 2025
item.dc:title: Quantum Kinetic Anatomy of Electron Angular Momenta Edge Accumulation

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: On-Demand and Tunable Andreev Conversion of Single-Electron Charge Pulses
item.description:
Author(s): Pablo Burset, Benjamin Roussel, Michael Moskalets, and Christian Flindt

Electron quantum optics explores coherent single-electron charge pulse propagation in electronic nanoscale circuits akin to tabletop photon setups. While past experiments focused on normal-state conductors, incorporating superconductors holds promise for exploiting the electron-hole degree of freedo…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246303] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pablo Burset, Benjamin Roussel, Michael Moskalets, and Christian Flindt

Electron quantum optics explores coherent single-electron charge pulse propagation in electronic nanoscale circuits akin to tabletop photon setups. While past experiments focused on normal-state conductors, incorporating superconductors holds promise for exploiting the electron-hole degree of freedo…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246303] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.dc:title: On-Demand and Tunable Andreev Conversion of Single-Electron Charge Pulses
item.dc:creator: Pablo Burset, Benjamin Roussel, Michael Moskalets, and Christian Flindt

item.title: Bidirectional Ultrafast Control of Charge Density Waves via Phase Competition
item.description:
Author(s): Honglie Ning, Kyoung Hun Oh, Yifan Su, Zhengyan Darius Shi, Dong Wu, Qiaomei Liu, B. Q. Lv, Alfred Zong, Gyeongbo Kang, Hyeongi Choi, Hyun-Woo J. Kim, Seunghyeok Ha, Jaehwon Kim, Suchismita Sarker, Jacob P. C. Ruff, B. J. Kim, N. L. Wang, Todadri Senthil, Hoyoung Jang, and Nuh Gedik

Competition between two charge density wave orders form a moiré superstructure, enabling bidirectional control of the charge density wave amplitude.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246504] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Honglie Ning, Kyoung Hun Oh, Yifan Su, Zhengyan Darius Shi, Dong Wu, Qiaomei Liu, B. Q. Lv, Alfred Zong, Gyeongbo Kang, Hyeongi Choi, Hyun-Woo J. Kim, Seunghyeok Ha, Jaehwon Kim, Suchismita Sarker, Jacob P. C. Ruff, B. J. Kim, N. L. Wang, Todadri Senthil, Hoyoung Jang, and Nuh Gedik

Competition between two charge density wave orders form a moiré superstructure, enabling bidirectional control of the charge density wave amplitude.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246504] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.dc:title: Bidirectional Ultrafast Control of Charge Density Waves via Phase Competition
item.dc:creator: Honglie Ning, Kyoung Hun Oh, Yifan Su, Zhengyan Darius Shi, Dong Wu, Qiaomei Liu, B. Q. Lv, Alfred Zong, Gyeongbo Kang, Hyeongi Choi, Hyun-Woo J. Kim, Seunghyeok Ha, Jaehwon Kim, Suchismita Sarker, Jacob P. C. Ruff, B. J. Kim, N. L. Wang, Todadri Senthil, Hoyoung Jang, and Nuh Gedik

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: THz-Driven Spin Dynamics in Orthoferrites with Kramers and Non-Kramers Rare-Earth Ions
item.description:
Author(s): R. A. Leenders, O. Y. Kovalenko, Y. Saito, N. R. Vovk, A. V. Kimel, and R. V. Mikhaylovskiy

We study the THz-driven spin dynamics of the canted antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{ErFeO}}_{3}$ across the spin reorientation temperatures 80–90 K. The amplitudes of the soft antiferromagnetic resonance mode are drastically enhanced by an order of magnitude in the middle of the spin reorientation tempera…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246703] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): R. A. Leenders, O. Y. Kovalenko, Y. Saito, N. R. Vovk, A. V. Kimel, and R. V. Mikhaylovskiy

We study the THz-driven spin dynamics of the canted antiferromagnet ErFeO3 across the spin reorientation temperatures 80–90 K. The amplitudes of the soft antiferromagnetic resonance mode are drastically enhanced by an order of magnitude in the middle of the spin reorientation temperature interval in…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246703] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.dc:title: THz-Driven Spin Dynamics in Orthoferrites with Kramers and Non-Kramers Rare-Earth Ions
item.dc:creator: R. A. Leenders, O. Y. Kovalenko, Y. Saito, N. R. Vovk, A. V. Kimel, and R. V. Mikhaylovskiy
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Gyrotropic Magnetic Effect in Metallic Chiral Magnets
item.description:
Author(s): Nisarga Paul, Takamori Park, Jung Hoon Han, and Leon Balents

We study the gyrotropic magnetic effect (GME), the low-frequency limit of optical gyrotropy, in metals and semimetals coupled to chiral spin textures. In these systems, the chiral spin texture which lacks inversion symmetry can imprint itself upon the electronic structure through Hund’s coupling, le…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246704] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nisarga Paul, Takamori Park, Jung Hoon Han, and Leon Balents

We study the gyrotropic magnetic effect (GME), the low-frequency limit of optical gyrotropy, in metals and semimetals coupled to chiral spin textures. In these systems, the chiral spin texture which lacks inversion symmetry can imprint itself upon the electronic structure through Hund’s coupling, le…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246704] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.dc:title: Gyrotropic Magnetic Effect in Metallic Chiral Magnets
item.dc:creator: Nisarga Paul, Takamori Park, Jung Hoon Han, and Leon Balents
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Inverse Acoustic Spin Hall Effect in Heavy Metal-Ferromagnet Bilayers
item.description:
Author(s): Yang Cao, Tong Li, Na Lei, Liyang Liao, Baoshan Cui, Li Xi, Dahai Wei, Tao Yu, Yoshichika Otani, Desheng Xue, and Dezheng Yang

The demonstration of wave conversion may lead to spintronic technology that transmits fragile spin data as acoustic waves.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246705] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yang Cao, Tong Li, Na Lei, Liyang Liao, Baoshan Cui, Li Xi, Dahai Wei, Tao Yu, Yoshichika Otani, Desheng Xue, and Dezheng Yang

The demonstration of wave conversion may lead to spintronic technology that transmits fragile spin data as acoustic waves.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246705] Published Fri Dec 12, 2025
item.dc:title: Inverse Acoustic Spin Hall Effect in Heavy Metal-Ferromagnet Bilayers
item.dc:creator: Yang Cao, Tong Li, Na Lei, Liyang Liao, Baoshan Cui, Li Xi, Dahai Wei, Tao Yu, Yoshichika Otani, Desheng Xue, and Dezheng Yang

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Bound States in the Continuum as Nodal Chain Points of Scattering Matrices
item.description:
Author(s): Wenzhe Liu, Yuan-Song Zeng, Jingyi Zhao, Chenfeng Yang, Ruo-Yang Zhang, Xiaohan Cui, Geng-Bo Wu, and C. T. Chan

Bound states in the continuum (BICs), exotic resonances with infinite lifetimes embedded in radiation continua, have long been studied for their topological robustness. Meanwhile, nodal lines—degenerate momentum-space manifolds in photonic and electronic band structures—represent another cornerstone…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 243804] Published Thu Dec 11, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wenzhe Liu, Yuan-Song Zeng, Jingyi Zhao, Chenfeng Yang, Ruo-Yang Zhang, Xiaohan Cui, Geng-Bo Wu, and C. T. Chan

Bound states in the continuum (BICs), exotic resonances with infinite lifetimes embedded in radiation continua, have long been studied for their topological robustness. Meanwhile, nodal lines—degenerate momentum-space manifolds in photonic and electronic band structures—represent another cornerstone…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 243804] Published Thu Dec 11, 2025
item.dc:title: Bound States in the Continuum as Nodal Chain Points of Scattering Matrices
item.dc:creator: Wenzhe Liu, Yuan-Song Zeng, Jingyi Zhao, Chenfeng Yang, Ruo-Yang Zhang, Xiaohan Cui, Geng-Bo Wu, and C. T. Chan
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Machine-Learning Prediction of Charged-Defect Formation Energies from Crystal Structures
item.description:
Author(s): Shin Kiyohara, Chisa Shibui, Soungmin Bae, and Yu Kumagai

Recent advances in materials informatics have expanded the number of synthesizable materials. However, screening promising candidates, such as semiconductors, based on defect properties remains challenging. This is primarily due to the lack of a general framework for predicting defect formation ener…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246101] Published Thu Dec 11, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shin Kiyohara, Chisa Shibui, Soungmin Bae, and Yu Kumagai

Recent advances in materials informatics have expanded the number of synthesizable materials. However, screening promising candidates, such as semiconductors, based on defect properties remains challenging. This is primarily due to the lack of a general framework for predicting defect formation ener…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246101] Published Thu Dec 11, 2025
item.dc:title: Machine-Learning Prediction of Charged-Defect Formation Energies from Crystal Structures
item.dc:creator: Shin Kiyohara, Chisa Shibui, Soungmin Bae, and Yu Kumagai
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Emergent Gauge Field in Composite-Fermion Metals: A Large-Scale Microscopic Study
item.description:
Author(s): Amogh Anakru, Mytraya Gattu, Ajit C. Balram, Xiao-Chuan Wu, Prashant Kumar, Zhen Bi, and J. K. Jain

Field theories of the composite-fermion (CF) metal model it as a Fermi sea of composite fermions coupled to an emergent gauge field. Within a random phase approximation, these theories predict that the Landau damping of the gauge field resulting from its coupling to the low-energy, long-wavelength C…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246503] Published Thu Dec 11, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Amogh Anakru, Mytraya Gattu, Ajit C. Balram, Xiao-Chuan Wu, Prashant Kumar, Zhen Bi, and J. K. Jain

Field theories of the composite-fermion (CF) metal model it as a Fermi sea of composite fermions coupled to an emergent gauge field. Within a random phase approximation, these theories predict that the Landau damping of the gauge field resulting from its coupling to the low-energy, long-wavelength C…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246503] Published Thu Dec 11, 2025
item.dc:title: Emergent Gauge Field in Composite-Fermion Metals: A Large-Scale Microscopic Study
item.dc:creator: Amogh Anakru, Mytraya Gattu, Ajit C. Balram, Xiao-Chuan Wu, Prashant Kumar, Zhen Bi, and J. K. Jain
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Theory of Magnetoroton Bands in Moiré Materials
item.description:
Author(s): Bishoy M. Kousa, Nicolás Morales-Durán, Tobias M. R. Wolf, Eslam Khalaf, and Allan H. MacDonald

The recent realization of Hofstadter spectra and fractional Chern insulators in moiré materials has introduced a new ingredient, a periodic lattice potential, to the study of quantum Hall phases. While the fractionalized states in moiré systems are expected to be in the same universality class as th…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246604] Published Thu Dec 11, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bishoy M. Kousa, Nicolás Morales-Durán, Tobias M. R. Wolf, Eslam Khalaf, and Allan H. MacDonald

The recent realization of Hofstadter spectra and fractional Chern insulators in moiré materials has introduced a new ingredient, a periodic lattice potential, to the study of quantum Hall phases. While the fractionalized states in moiré systems are expected to be in the same universality class as th…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246604] Published Thu Dec 11, 2025
item.dc:title: Theory of Magnetoroton Bands in Moiré Materials
item.dc:creator: Bishoy M. Kousa, Nicolás Morales-Durán, Tobias M. R. Wolf, Eslam Khalaf, and Allan H. MacDonald

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Magneto-Optical Trapping of Aluminum Monofluoride
item.description:
Author(s): J. E. Padilla-Castillo, J. Cai, P. Agarwal, P. Kukreja, R. Thomas, B. G. Sartakov, S. Truppe, G. Meijer, and S. C. Wright

A class of molecules with two valence electrons has been laser cooled and trapped for the first time.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 243401] Published Wed Dec 10, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): J. E. Padilla-Castillo, J. Cai, P. Agarwal, P. Kukreja, R. Thomas, B. G. Sartakov, S. Truppe, G. Meijer, and S. C. Wright

A class of molecules with two valence electrons has been laser cooled and trapped for the first time.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 243401] Published Wed Dec 10, 2025
item.dc:title: Magneto-Optical Trapping of Aluminum Monofluoride
item.dc:creator: J. E. Padilla-Castillo, J. Cai, P. Agarwal, P. Kukreja, R. Thomas, B. G. Sartakov, S. Truppe, G. Meijer, and S. C. Wright

item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Optical Injection and Detection of Long-Lived Interlayer Excitons in van der Waals Heterostructures
item.description:
Author(s): Alperen Tüğen, Anna M. Seiler, Arthur Christianen, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Martin Kroner, and Ataç İmamoğlu

Interlayer excitons in semiconducting bilayers separated by insulating hexagonal boron nitride ($h$-BN) layers constitute a promising platform for investigation of strongly correlated bosonic phases. Here, we report an optical method for the generation and characterization of long-lived interlayer e…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246502] Published Wed Dec 10, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Alperen Tüğen, Anna M. Seiler, Arthur Christianen, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Martin Kroner, and Ataç İmamoğlu

Interlayer excitons in semiconducting bilayers separated by insulating hexagonal boron nitride (h-BN) layers constitute a promising platform for investigation of strongly correlated bosonic phases. Here, we report an optical method for the generation and characterization of long-lived interlayer exc…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246502] Published Wed Dec 10, 2025
item.dc:title: Optical Injection and Detection of Long-Lived Interlayer Excitons in van der Waals Heterostructures
item.dc:creator: Alperen Tüğen, Anna M. Seiler, Arthur Christianen, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Martin Kroner, and Ataç İmamoğlu

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Nonstabilizerness Dynamics in Many-Body Localized Systems
item.description:
Author(s): Pedro R. Nicácio Falcão, Piotr Sierant, Jakub Zakrzewski, and Emanuele Tirrito

Nonstabilizerness, also known as “magic,” quantifies the deviation of quantum states from stabilizer states, capturing the complexity necessary for quantum computational advantage. In this Letter, we investigate the dynamics of nonstabilizerness in disordered many-body localized (MBL) systems using …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 240404] Published Tue Dec 09, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pedro R. Nicácio Falcão, Piotr Sierant, Jakub Zakrzewski, and Emanuele Tirrito

Nonstabilizerness, also known as “magic,” quantifies the deviation of quantum states from stabilizer states, capturing the complexity necessary for quantum computational advantage. In this Letter, we investigate the dynamics of nonstabilizerness in disordered many-body localized (MBL) systems using …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 240404] Published Tue Dec 09, 2025
item.dc:title: Nonstabilizerness Dynamics in Many-Body Localized Systems
item.dc:creator: Pedro R. Nicácio Falcão, Piotr Sierant, Jakub Zakrzewski, and Emanuele Tirrito
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Direct Reconstruction of Terahertz-Driven Subcycle Electron Emission Dynamics
item.description:
Author(s): Jiakang Mao, Yushan Zeng, Hongyang Li, Liwei Song, Ye Tian, and Ruxin Li

While field-driven electron emission is theoretically understood down to the subcycle regime, its direct experimental temporal characterization using long-wavelength terahertz (THz) fields remains elusive. Here, by driving a graphite tip with phase-stable quasi-single-cycle THz pulses, we reveal dis…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 243803] Published Tue Dec 09, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jiakang Mao, Yushan Zeng, Hongyang Li, Liwei Song, Ye Tian, and Ruxin Li

While field-driven electron emission is theoretically understood down to the subcycle regime, its direct experimental temporal characterization using long-wavelength terahertz (THz) fields remains elusive. Here, by driving a graphite tip with phase-stable quasi-single-cycle THz pulses, we reveal dis…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 243803] Published Tue Dec 09, 2025
item.dc:title: Direct Reconstruction of Terahertz-Driven Subcycle Electron Emission Dynamics
item.dc:creator: Jiakang Mao, Yushan Zeng, Hongyang Li, Liwei Song, Ye Tian, and Ruxin Li
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Topological Chiral Superconductivity Mediated by Intervalley Antiferromagnetic Fluctuations in Twisted Bilayer ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Wei Qin, Wen-Xuan Qiu, and Fengcheng Wu

Motivated by the recent observations of superconductivity in twisted bilayer ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ (${\mathrm{tWSe}}_{2}$), we theoretically investigate the superconductivity driven by an electronic mechanism. We first demonstrate that the multiband screened Coulomb interaction within the random phas…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246002] Published Tue Dec 09, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wei Qin, Wen-Xuan Qiu, and Fengcheng Wu

Motivated by the recent observations of superconductivity in twisted bilayer WSe2 (tWSe2), we theoretically investigate the superconductivity driven by an electronic mechanism. We first demonstrate that the multiband screened Coulomb interaction within the random phase approximation is insufficient …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246002] Published Tue Dec 09, 2025
item.dc:title: Topological Chiral Superconductivity Mediated by Intervalley Antiferromagnetic Fluctuations in Twisted Bilayer ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Wei Qin, Wen-Xuan Qiu, and Fengcheng Wu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Two-Dimensional Superconducting Diode Effect in Topological Insulator/Superconductor Heterostructure
item.description:
Author(s): Soma Nagahama, Yuki Sato, Minoru Kawamura, Ilya Belopolski, Ryutaro Yoshimi, Atsushi Tsukazaki, Naoya Kanazawa, Kei S. Takahashi, Masashi Kawasaki, and Yoshinori Tokura

The superconducting diode effect (SDE) is characterized by the nonreciprocity of Cooper-pair motion with respect to current direction. In three-dimensional (3D) materials, SDE results in a critical current that varies with direction, making the effect distinctly observable: the material exhibits sup…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246003] Published Tue Dec 09, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Soma Nagahama, Yuki Sato, Minoru Kawamura, Ilya Belopolski, Ryutaro Yoshimi, Atsushi Tsukazaki, Naoya Kanazawa, Kei S. Takahashi, Masashi Kawasaki, and Yoshinori Tokura

The superconducting diode effect (SDE) is characterized by the nonreciprocity of Cooper-pair motion with respect to current direction. In three-dimensional (3D) materials, SDE results in a critical current that varies with direction, making the effect distinctly observable: the material exhibits sup…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246003] Published Tue Dec 09, 2025
item.dc:title: Two-Dimensional Superconducting Diode Effect in Topological Insulator/Superconductor Heterostructure
item.dc:creator: Soma Nagahama, Yuki Sato, Minoru Kawamura, Ilya Belopolski, Ryutaro Yoshimi, Atsushi Tsukazaki, Naoya Kanazawa, Kei S. Takahashi, Masashi Kawasaki, and Yoshinori Tokura

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Field-Effect-Tunable Even-Odd Transition of Quantum Hall States in a Rashba System
item.description:
Author(s): Qijia Xu, Jingyue Wang, Junwei Huang, Yufei Zhao, Weiyu Sun, Xuzhong Cong, Huakun Zuo, Zengwei Zhu, Congwei Tan, Hongtao Liu, Binghai Yan, Hongtao Yuan, and Hailin Peng

The quantum Hall effect is one of the most fundamental quantum phenomena in condensed matter physics, and via tuning the quantum Hall states (QHSs), the evolution of band topologies and electron correlations can be investigated and revealed therein. However, for the vast majority of QHS systems, eve…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246302] Published Tue Dec 09, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Qijia Xu, Jingyue Wang, Junwei Huang, Yufei Zhao, Weiyu Sun, Xuzhong Cong, Huakun Zuo, Zengwei Zhu, Congwei Tan, Hongtao Liu, Binghai Yan, Hongtao Yuan, and Hailin Peng

The quantum Hall effect is one of the most fundamental quantum phenomena in condensed matter physics, and via tuning the quantum Hall states (QHSs), the evolution of band topologies and electron correlations can be investigated and revealed therein. However, for the vast majority of QHS systems, eve…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246302] Published Tue Dec 09, 2025
item.dc:title: Field-Effect-Tunable Even-Odd Transition of Quantum Hall States in a Rashba System
item.dc:creator: Qijia Xu, Jingyue Wang, Junwei Huang, Yufei Zhao, Weiyu Sun, Xuzhong Cong, Huakun Zuo, Zengwei Zhu, Congwei Tan, Hongtao Liu, Binghai Yan, Hongtao Yuan, and Hailin Peng
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Fractional Quantum Hall State at $ν\text{}=\text{}1/2$ with Energy Gap Up to 6 K and Possible Transition from the One- to Two-Component State
item.description:
Author(s): Siddharth Kumar Singh, Chengyu Wang, Adbhut Gupta, Kirk W. Baldwin, Loren N. Pfeiffer, and Mansour Shayegan

The fractional quantum Hall state (FQHS) observed in the lowest Landau level at filling factor $ν=1/2$ in wide quantum wells has been enigmatic for decades because the two-dimensional electron system (2DES) has a bilayer charge distribution but with significant interlayer tunneling. Of particular in…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246603] Published Tue Dec 09, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Siddharth Kumar Singh, Chengyu Wang, Adbhut Gupta, Kirk W. Baldwin, Loren N. Pfeiffer, and Mansour Shayegan

The fractional quantum Hall state (FQHS) observed in the lowest Landau level at filling factor ν=1/2 in wide quantum wells has been enigmatic for decades because the two-dimensional electron system (2DES) has a bilayer charge distribution but with significant interlayer tunneling. Of particular inte…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246603] Published Tue Dec 09, 2025
item.dc:title: Fractional Quantum Hall State at $ν\text{}=\text{}1/2$ with Energy Gap Up to 6 K and Possible Transition from the One- to Two-Component State
item.dc:creator: Siddharth Kumar Singh, Chengyu Wang, Adbhut Gupta, Kirk W. Baldwin, Loren N. Pfeiffer, and Mansour Shayegan
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Twofold-Symmetric Magnetoelasticity Induced by Dominant Vertical Shear Strain
item.description:
Author(s): Fa Chen, Liyang Liao, Jiaxin Chen, Qiuyun Fu, Yue Zhang, Wei Luo, and Yoshichika Otani

We report an unconventional twofold-symmetric magnetoelastic coupling in Ni films, mediated by Rayleigh surface acoustic waves (SAWs). This unique magnetoelastic symmetry originates from a dominant vertical shear strain ${ϵ}_{yz}$, which becomes prominent due to the low effective elastic modulus of …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246702] Published Tue Dec 09, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Fa Chen, Liyang Liao, Jiaxin Chen, Qiuyun Fu, Yue Zhang, Wei Luo, and Yoshichika Otani

We report an unconventional twofold-symmetric magnetoelastic coupling in Ni films, mediated by Rayleigh surface acoustic waves (SAWs). This unique magnetoelastic symmetry originates from a dominant vertical shear strain ϵyz, which becomes prominent due to the low effective elastic modulus of Ni film…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246702] Published Tue Dec 09, 2025
item.dc:title: Twofold-Symmetric Magnetoelasticity Induced by Dominant Vertical Shear Strain
item.dc:creator: Fa Chen, Liyang Liao, Jiaxin Chen, Qiuyun Fu, Yue Zhang, Wei Luo, and Yoshichika Otani
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Multicolor Phonon Excitation in Terahertz Cavities
item.description:
Author(s): Omer Yaniv and Dominik M. Juraschek

Driving materials using light with more than one frequency component is an emerging technique, enabled by advanced pulse-shaping capabilities in recent years. Here, we translate this technique to lattice vibrations by exciting multicolor phonons using terahertz cavities. In contrast to light, phonon…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246901] Published Tue Dec 09, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Omer Yaniv and Dominik M. Juraschek

Driving materials using light with more than one frequency component is an emerging technique, enabled by advanced pulse-shaping capabilities in recent years. Here, we translate this technique to lattice vibrations by exciting multicolor phonons using terahertz cavities. In contrast to light, phonon…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246901] Published Tue Dec 09, 2025
item.dc:title: Multicolor Phonon Excitation in Terahertz Cavities
item.dc:creator: Omer Yaniv and Dominik M. Juraschek
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: From Kardar-Parisi-Zhang Scaling to Soliton Proliferation in Josephson Junction Arrays
item.description:
Author(s): Mikheil Tsitsishvili, Reinhold Egger, Karsten Flensberg, and Sebastian Diehl

We propose Josephson junction arrays as realistic platforms for observing nonequilibrium scaling laws characterizing the Kardar-Parisi-Zhang (KPZ) universality class, and space-time soliton proliferation. Focusing on a two-chain ladder geometry, we perform numerical simulations for the roughness fun…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246001] Published Mon Dec 08, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mikheil Tsitsishvili, Reinhold Egger, Karsten Flensberg, and Sebastian Diehl

We propose Josephson junction arrays as realistic platforms for observing nonequilibrium scaling laws characterizing the Kardar-Parisi-Zhang (KPZ) universality class, and space-time soliton proliferation. Focusing on a two-chain ladder geometry, we perform numerical simulations for the roughness fun…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246001] Published Mon Dec 08, 2025
item.dc:title: From Kardar-Parisi-Zhang Scaling to Soliton Proliferation in Josephson Junction Arrays
item.dc:creator: Mikheil Tsitsishvili, Reinhold Egger, Karsten Flensberg, and Sebastian Diehl
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Luttinger Count is the Homotopy Not the Physical Charge: Generalized Anomalies Characterize Non-Fermi Liquids
item.description:
Author(s): Gabriele La Nave, Jinchao Zhao, and Philip W. Phillips

We show that the Luttinger-Ward functional can be formulated as an operator insertion in the path integral and hence can be thought of as a generalized symmetry. The key result is that the associated charge, always quantized, defines the homotopy, not the physical charge. The disconnect between the …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246501] Published Mon Dec 08, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gabriele La Nave, Jinchao Zhao, and Philip W. Phillips

We show that the Luttinger-Ward functional can be formulated as an operator insertion in the path integral and hence can be thought of as a generalized symmetry. The key result is that the associated charge, always quantized, defines the homotopy, not the physical charge. The disconnect between the …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246501] Published Mon Dec 08, 2025
item.dc:title: Luttinger Count is the Homotopy Not the Physical Charge: Generalized Anomalies Characterize Non-Fermi Liquids
item.dc:creator: Gabriele La Nave, Jinchao Zhao, and Philip W. Phillips
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Interplay of Silver-Mean Quasiperiodicity and Weyl Semimetal Phase in ${\mathrm{WTe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): So-Dam Sohn, Ja-Yong Koo, Chang-Youn Moon, and Daejin Eom

Scanning tunneling microscopy reveals that epoxy resin intercalates into a 3D Weyl semimetal with molecular-scale height modulations that follow the Pell sequence.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246601] Published Mon Dec 08, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): So-Dam Sohn, Ja-Yong Koo, Chang-Youn Moon, and Daejin Eom

Scanning tunneling microscopy reveals that epoxy resin intercalates into a 3D Weyl semimetal with molecular-scale height modulations that follow the Pell sequence.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246601] Published Mon Dec 08, 2025
item.dc:title: Interplay of Silver-Mean Quasiperiodicity and Weyl Semimetal Phase in ${\mathrm{WTe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: So-Dam Sohn, Ja-Yong Koo, Chang-Youn Moon, and Daejin Eom
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Edge-State Selective Measurement of Dispersions in the Quantum Hall Regime
item.description:
Author(s): Henok Weldeyesus, T. Patlatiuk, Q. Chen, C. P. Scheller, D. M. Zumbühl, A. Yacoby, L. N. Pfeiffer, and K. W. West

A new method utilizes a GaAs 2D electron gas, combined with cleaved-edge overgrowth quantum wires, to accurately map the dispersions and velocities of individual edge states across a wide range of parameters.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246602] Published Mon Dec 08, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Henok Weldeyesus, T. Patlatiuk, Q. Chen, C. P. Scheller, D. M. Zumbühl, A. Yacoby, L. N. Pfeiffer, and K. W. West

A new method utilizes a GaAs 2D electron gas, combined with cleaved-edge overgrowth quantum wires, to accurately map the dispersions and velocities of individual edge states across a wide range of parameters.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 246602] Published Mon Dec 08, 2025
item.dc:title: Edge-State Selective Measurement of Dispersions in the Quantum Hall Regime
item.dc:creator: Henok Weldeyesus, T. Patlatiuk, Q. Chen, C. P. Scheller, D. M. Zumbühl, A. Yacoby, L. N. Pfeiffer, and K. W. West
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials
